calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-22-2022 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendants Lifenews.Com Llc, And Steven Ertelt'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-15-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT LIFENEWS.COM LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendants Lifenews.Com Llc, And Steven Ertelt'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or Summary Adjudication.

Off calendar. Request for dismissal of entire action filed 8/23/22.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-28-2022 Set For Trial On Oct-17-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-20-2022 Continued To Mar-23-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Jan-27-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Aimee Kirby, Esq. (415.421.2800; aimee.kirby@dolanlawfirm.com) of Dolan Law Firm, PC for Plaintiff.
Jessica Pliner, Esq. (925.357.3445; Jessica.pliner@lewisbrisbois.com) of Lewis Brisbois for specially appearing Defendants.

A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANTS LIFENEWS.COM LLC and STEVEN ERTELT'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS.

Defendants' "motion to quash summons for lack of personal jurisdiction" is denied.

Defendants posted a photograph of plaintiff in police garb with an article about a proposed California law on their pro-life website. Plaintiff sued in this court for defamation, false light and negligence.

As the parties agree, California "allows the exercise of personal jurisdiction to the fullest extent permissible under the U.S. Constitution." (Memo. 4:6-7.) The parties also agree that California's three-part test for specific jurisdiction applies. (Pavlovich v. Sup. Ct. (2002) 29 Cal.4th 262, 269.)

Defendants concede that 8% of their 1.5 million pageviews are in California, that they have successfully solicited donations by mail to 400-some Californians in California, that they have solicited advertisements from at least two California entities and that they "use advertisements from Goggle, a California company." (Memo. 2:28-3:12, 11:21.) This suffices to show that defendants "purposefully availed" themselves of California contacts that were not "random, fortuitous or attenuated." (Pavlovich, 29 Cal.4th at 269.) Moreover, the "controversy is related to" defendants' reporting to Californians on a proposed California law, so personal jurisdiction comports with "fair play and substantial justice." (Id.; Memo. 11:2-4.)

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
